parts.text: Chrome extensions stopped loading on all devices in the Marketing OU after yesterday's update. About 30 users are affected.
I'll route this to the Support Intake workflow. This is an EXTENSION issue at P2 priority (team-wide, 30 users). Running Triage → Diagnose → Resolve.
Enable Safe Browsing Enhanced Protection for the entire organization and disable incognito mode for the Finance OU.

I'll use the Policy Automation workflow to draft and validate these two policy changes. Running Draft ↔ Validate loop.

Policy 1: SafeBrowsingProtectionLevel = 2 (Enhanced) at root OU
Policy 2: IncognitoModeAvailability = 1 (Disabled) at /Finance OU
We need a compliance review before our SOC2 audit next month. Check our Chrome fleet DLP and extension policies.
I'll run the Security Posture workflow which performs DLP, Extension, and Compliance audits concurrently. This will check your configuration against CIS benchmarks and enterprise security baselines.
What are the steps to set up Chrome Browser Cloud Management on Windows laptops?

For Chrome Browser Cloud Management (CBCM) on Windows:

1. Admin Console → Devices → Chrome → Managed Browsers
2. Generate enrollment token: Settings → Enrollment Token
3. Deploy via Windows Registry key CloudManagementEnrollmentToken
4. Chrome auto-enrolls on next launch
5. Verify in Admin Console under Managed Browsers
Create a runbook for our team's quarterly Chrome extension audit process.

I'll use the runbook-creator skill to generate a structured operational runbook for your quarterly Chrome extension audit. The runbook will include prerequisites, step-by-step procedures, validation checks, and escalation paths.
How many workflows do you support?

I support 3 main workflows and 4 knowledge skills:

1. Support Intake (Sequential) — Triage, diagnose, and resolve Chrome/ChromeOS issues
2. Policy Automation (Loop) — Draft and validate policy changes iteratively
3. Security Posture (Parallel) — Run DLP, extension, and compliance audits concurrently

Plus 4 skills: chrome-policy-quick-ref, chrome-policy-reference, enrollment-playbook, and runbook-creator.
